title: Deliciously Smoky Norwegian Mulukhiyah Stew
description: A hearty and smoky Norwegian stew featuring mulukhiyah, perfect for a lazy Sunday, and keto-friendly. This dish is slow-cooked to bring out the rich flavors of the ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb shoulder with smoked paprika and let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
In a large Dutch oven, sear the lamb shoulder over medium-high heat until browned on all sides.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the mulukhiyah, beef broth, and browned lamb shoulder to the pot, making sure that the lamb is mostly submerged.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the stew for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and falling off the bone.
Stir in the heavy cream and cook for an additional 10 minutes, until the stew has thickened slightly.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
